subject: Geology
title: Basalt
description: A micro-lesson that makes Basalt usable.

hook.text: The ocean floor is almost entirely basalt. It's the most common rock on Earth, and most of it is underwater.

buildup.text: Basalt is a dark, fine-grained extrusive igneous rock formed from fast-cooling lava at the surface.

discovery.text: It erupts at mid-ocean ridges, building new ocean crust. Hawaii and Iceland are built almost entirely of basalt.

twist.text: Basalt is also the dominant rock on Mars, Venus, and the Moon. It's the most common rock in the solar system.

climax.text: Basalt paves the ocean floors, builds volcanic islands, and covers other planets. It is truly universal rock.

punchline.text: The universe's favorite rock is basalt.

quiz.question: Where does most basalt on Earth form?
options: At mid-ocean ridges from fast-cooling lava
